prompts: Time dilation is a concept from Einstein's theory of relativity, explaining how time can appear to pass at different rates for observers in different states of motion. According to relativity, as an object approaches the speed of light, time for that object appears to slow down relative to a stationary observer. This means that if two identical clocks are synchronized, and one is moving at high speed while the other remains stationary, the moving clock will tick slower than the stationary one. This effect is a consequence of the invariant nature of the speed of light across all reference frames, leading to differing perceptions of time.
